title: silene inflata subsp. vulgaris
intro: Silene inflata subsp. vulgaris, also known as Bladder Campion, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Silene inflata subsp. vulgaris include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
